The Lego Approach to Data Transformation: Building Modular DBT Pipelines at Scale

😎It's here! Our next dbt meetup is coming up and will be hosted at the DoubleVerify office:

28 Crosby St, floor 6
New York, NY 10013

We are looking forward to socializing and seeing all of the new and familiar faces!

💡dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

Today, we'll explore the performance implications of abstraction at scale, share lessons on getting teams to embrace reusable components over custom implementations, and discuss practical strategies for identifying what to abstract. Learn how our modular approach reduced new data source onboarding from 3 weeks to 2 days while ensuring consistent metrics across all pipelines.

If you're struggling with metric consistency or drowning in duplicated transformation logic, you'll leave with concrete patterns to make your DBT projects more maintainable and scalable.

🏠Venue Host: DoubleVerify
🍕Catering: TBD
🤝Organizer: Brooklyn Data + DoubleVerify
📣 Agenda and additional details below:

6:30pm Intro remarks | Refreshments & Conversation
7:00-7:25pm Aravind Ramesh + Mahima Arya Q&A (see topic below)
7:30-7:40pm Closing remarks
7:45-9:00pm Swag, Reception & Networking

✨Speakers and Topic:

Aravind Ramesh, Sr. Dir Software Engineer and Mahima Arya, Sr. Manager, Software Engineer at DoubleVerify, will dive into The Lego Approach to Data Transformation: Building Modular DBT Pipelines at Scale

Our Host: At DoubleVerify, we process billions of ad events from dozens of sources, constantly rebuilding the same complex transformations—fraud detection, viewability, and brand-safety metrics—for every new pipeline. This talk shows how we applied core programming principles (abstraction, modularity, DRY) to transform scattered SQL into reusable DBT packages. Like Lego blocks with standard connections, we built a three-layer architecture: raw data, standardized signals, and modular packages that work with any conforming input.

Our Tool: dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come and reach out to amsal.alic@velir.com if you need to cancel at the last minute. At any time, you may update your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

All participants are welcome. For questions regarding access and accommodation, please contact amsal.alic@velir.com in advance.

Read more
Speaker
Aravind Ramesh, Sr. Dir Software Engineer & Mahima Arya, Sr. Manager, Software
Aravind Ramesh is a Senior Director of Software Engineering at DoubleVerify, leading platform integrations and data quality for social measurement products. He drives strategic projects that reduce time-to-market, improve data reliability, and advance DoubleVerify’s social measurement capabilities.
Mahima Arya is a Sr. Manager of Software Engineering at DoubleVerify, managing multiple partners' integrations for the Social Department. She was the first in the Social Integrations team to utilize DBT with Reddit, Roblox, LinkedIn, and many others.

In this meetup, we’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas we’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.

🤝Organizer:dbt Labs's Community and Developer Experience (DX) teams
🏠Venue: 873 Broadway, 2nd floor (b/w 18th and 19th St)

📝Agenda

5:30 - 6:00 | Check in/Registration
6:00 - 6:05 | Welcome Remarks by Community Team
6:05 - 6:35 | Presentation 1: “For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs” & Q&A by Jason Ganz (dbt Labs)
6:35 - 8:30 | Networking & Reception

EVENT DETAILS:
To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation:** https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

The doors open at 5:30pm. We kindly ask that you not arrive before then, as we may not be able to accommodate you.

Our venue has capacity limits, so please RSVP if you intend to come. If you need to cancel at the last minute, please change your RSVP status on this page to "Not Going."

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝 Make sure to join the #local-nyc channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

***

Since 2016, dbt Labs has been on a mission to help data practitioners create and disseminate organizational knowledge. dbt is the standard for AI-ready structured data. Powered by the dbt Fusion engine, it unlocks the performance, context, and trust that organizations need to scale analytics in the era of AI. Globally, more than 60,000 data teams use dbt, including those at Siemens, Roche and Condé Nast.
Learn more at getdbt.com, and follow dbt Labs on LinkedIn, X, Instagram, and YouTube.

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

We're very excited to be hosting our first IN-PERSON NYC dbt meetup in over 2 years! Looking forward to socializing and seeing new and familiar faces.

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come and and reach out to partnerships@brooklyndata.co if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-nyc channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

***

📣 Agenda and additional details below:

6:30pm Intro remarks | Refreshments & Conversation
7:00-7:25 Talk #1 + Q&A
7:25-7:50 Talk #2 + Q&A
7:50-8:00pm Closing remarks
8:00-9:00 Reception & Networking (socializing, light food and refreshments 🍷 🍺 🧀 ☕ 🥤)

✨ Speaker Lineup✨

🗣 Aaron Richter, Senior Data Engineer at Squarespace

A Deep Dive into the dbt Manifest

Ever noticed the manifest.json file that dbt puts into your target folder? This little file contains rich information about your dbt project that enables numerous fun use cases! These include complex deployment configurations, quality enforcement, and streamlined development workflows. This talk will go over what the manifest is and how it is produced, along with case studies of how the manifest is used across Squarespace’s data pipelines.

This talk will be accessible to new dabblers in dbt all the way to seasoned experts. Come find out what the manifest can do for you!

🗣 Sam Werbalowsky, Senior Data Engineer at TeePublic

Creating a Smooth Transition from Data Person to Data Team

Have you ever been the “data person” at a company, and suddenly had to hire a data or analytics engineer? Sam will talk about shifting a team from a team of one to a team of two. Some highlights include establishing standards for syntax and modeling, finding operational inefficiencies, and reducing internalized knowledge.

👆 Stay tuned for more details on these talks!

***

dbt is a command line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.

🗣 Emily Steffen Batchos, Manager, Analytics at Dandy

Measuring Product Adoption: How we've used dbt to provide actionable insights

Emily will cover how her team has used dbt to provide actionable insights. Some highlights of the talk will be frequent stakeholder questions she asks to get here and why she determined she needed a reporting data layer. She will also be demoing some of the visualizations and insights that she's able to get by doing a second level index on dataspine and what that means in terms of helpful comparisons.

🗣 Evan Johnson, VP of Analytics and Data Strategy at Steady

How Steady is Overhauling Data Productization Technology to Broaden Reach and Improve Iteration

Steady is a robo income advisor for the modern gig economy workforce which leverages its unique income intelligence engine to deliver proprietary insights to 110 million American gig workers. Evan will talk about how their Growth, Data, Product, and Engineering teams are currently overhauling the data productization technology and process to broaden reach and improve iteration by leveraging Snowflake, Ascend, Kafka, Braze, Airflow, Databricks, and Elastic Search. This will allow Steady to more easily prototype and harden the most valuable insights for Steady members, by giving them the ability to send analytic data sets directly to the Steady mobile app via a production database.

Join hosts Brooklyn Data Co and speakers from DonorsChoose and WeWork.

For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-nyc channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

-----------------------------------

✨ Speaker Lineup✨

🗣 Mohammad Radiyat, Data Scientist at DonorsChoose

Making Equity Transparent: Using dbt to Secure our Demographic Data

DonorsChoose is a national nonprofit that makes it easy for anyone to help a teacher in need, moving us closer to a nation where students in every community have the tools and experiences they need for a great education. Mohammad will cover how the data team at DonorsChoose allowed thier colleagues to access aggregated equity data while securing their teacher demographic data using dbt. Some highlights of the talk will include using macros to keep transformations DRY, the awesomeness of incremental models, and the ease of dbt Cloud in managing their dbt jobs.

🗣 Raify Hidalgo, Data Engineer & Max Kang Senior, Data Engineer

Building a Unified Streaming and Batch Pipeline with DBT and Snowflake

Raify and Max will dive into the implementation of a batch and streaming pipeline they developed at WeWork, which leverages dbt and Snowflake. They aimed to use their existing stack in order to optimize for speed to delivery, along with minimal debugging time, and minimal learning curves. This was their first foray into the world of streaming data modeling, where they owned most of the end-to-end pipeline. You'll hear all about the considerations they made, what they learned, challenges they faced, and why - in the end - they wouldn't have been able to get up and running without developing on their existing stack: Airflow, dbt & Snowflake!

✨ Speaker Lineup✨

🗣 Sonia Mehta, Director of Analytics at Elevate Labs

Building Your QA Framework

QA can include many things and it’s easy to go down a rabbit hole of all the ways to test your data for completion and accuracy. Depending on the error, what to do next can vary greatly as well! In this talk, Sonia will aim to talk about the types of tests to apply and how to decide on acceptable thresholds for failures. She'll also walk through how dbt has helped Elevate Labs have more reliable and accurate data.

🗣 Lauren Talbot, VP of Data & Analytics at BARK

How BARK’s Data Team Created a Feedback Loop Between BI and Data Science

With a fast-growing data team spread across many locations, BARK wanted to make sure they were collaborating and knowledge-sharing efficiently. In this talk, Lauren will share how she worked with her team to create a knowledge repo -- an all-in-one solution that could document, share, keep track of insights, and build upon those insights iteratively, without ever having to reinvent the wheel.

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #events-nyc channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------------

Agenda ✅

12:00 pm - 12:05 pm: Welcome Remarks & Attendee Intros
12:05 pm - 12:35 pm: dbt For Progress: Using dbt + Data Engineering for November's Election
12:35 pm - 12:55 pm: Q&A
12:55 pm - 1:00 pm: Closing Remarks

----------------------------------------

Speaker & Presentation Details

🎤 dbt For Progress: Using dbt + Data Engineering for November's Election
Jason Ganz, Chief Technology Officer, Data for Progress

Data for Progress is a progressive think tank focused on using data to drive progressive change. Their main service to the movement is polling and public opinion data. This talk will show how:

dbt helps them track their KPIs over time and by group
dbt allows them to ensure that different types of complex surveys are accurate and representative
a culture of data engineering + analytics engineering — over fancy machine — learning has allowed us to drive maximum impact
----------------------------------------

Welcome to the first online-only NYC Meetup! We are disappointed we can't meet in person, but are still looking forward to seeing your friendly faces on Zoom.

For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #events-nyc channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------------
Speaker Lineup

Speakers:
Katie Claiborne, Data Scientist, Cityblock Health
Tanjin Panna, Site Reliability Engineer, Cityblock Health

Presentation:
Improving Reliability and Orchestration with dbt

Description:
Last year, the Cityblock team rolled out their dbt project. Happy days, right? Not quite. Occasionally, the dreaded slack message "the dashboards are stale," would greet them in the morning and their team was challenged to find solutions to prevent this from happening. In this talk, Katie and Tanjin will run through some of the ways their team improved the orchestration of their data stack to increase the uptime of their dashboards, so they could start their mornings on the right track! Stack: BigQuery, dbt, Airflow, Looker

--------

Speakers:
Nick McCormick, Senior Manager, Analytics Engineering, Bombas🧦
Kevin Kong, Business Intelligence Analyst, Bombas🧦

Presentation:
Driving Data Curiosity with Analytics Engineering

Description:
When our founders wanted to make the most comfortable socks in the history of feet, they started with the very first data point at Bombas – "Socks are the #1 most requested item at a homeless shelter". From there, we've grown our data tremendously, and the Analytics Engineering team has been a key part of maintaining a data-driven culture.

----------------------------------------

New York dbt Meetup

Details

Come join us at Bowery Farming (https://boweryfarming.com/) for the first dbt Meetup of the new year!

This event is open to all folks working with data—while we'll predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, we're also very interested in broader topics related to analytics engineering, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

Doors open at 6:30pm, for a 6:45 start! We'll bring the food and drinks, just make sure you have your ID to sign in!

----------------------------------------
Migrating 387 dbt models from Redshift to Snowflake
The Bowery Farming Data Team 👩‍🌾

Bowery Farming have just completed their transition from Redshift to Snowflake, and along the way have picked up some things to share with the dbt community. If you're considering switching warehouse vendors, learn how Bowery took on this project: from how long it took, to the parts that were trickier than expected, and the tools they built to make make the process easier.

----------------------------------------

How to hire an analytics engineer: a candidate’s perspective
Danielle Leong

Hiring is hard, especially for an emerging role like that of an analytics engineer. In this talk, Danielle will share her experience as a candidate, from the resources she used to find roles (and where you should be posting as hiring manager), to the interviews and evaluations that she found most useful.

----------------------------------------

Using dbt + Machine Learning to predict customer conversions
Kenny Ning, Data Engineer
Better.com 🏠

If you’re currently thinking about how to use machine learning to make inferences about your business, this talk is for you. At better.com, the team is obsessed with understanding what makes borrowers go from submitting a mortgage application to ultimately funding with better.com as their direct lender. In this talk, Kenny will share the “full-stack data science” approach they took to building a model to predict conversions (hint: it features dbt).

----------------------------------------

dbt Meetup at Warby Parker

Hosted by Claire C.

New York dbt Meetup
4.7

Details

This event is hosted by Warby Parker (www.warbyparker.com) and open to all folks working with data—while we'll predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, we're also very interested in broader topics related to analytics engineering, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

Doors open at 6pm, for a 6:30 start! We'll bring the food and drinks, just make sure you have your ID to sign in!

----------------------------------------
Speaker Lineup

Sean McIntytre, Lead Data Engineer
👓Warby Parker
Ever wondered how dbt actually works? At Warby Parker, the team had a look under the hood.
In this presentation, learn about the inner workings of dbt, and how anyone can build new functionality in dbt just by writing code in their own dbt project. Warby Parker will also share some of the ways they have extended dbt, and how they contributed that code back to the community, making dbt better for all of us.

Andie DeLeón, Data & Analytics
🏃‍♀️P.volve
You may have heard the term "single view of the customer" before. Before you get there, you need to be able to stitch together all your users from the several different SaaS tools your business is using. Sounds easy right? What about when some sources have user_ids, others have emails, and others only have a tracking cookie? Learn how P.volve built a "unified users" model, where one record represented (their best guess) of an IRL person, by joining together several data sources.

Joe Naso, Technical Product Manager
🧠 pymetrics
You've all heard the talks about how wonderful things are after the implementation of new technology, but they tend to skim over how bumpy the ride there can be. pymetrics rolled out is first data warehouse only to find it moving quickly from an afterthought to the primary data source for a number of client-facing products. Learn about all the pitfalls and mistakes the team made during implementation and roll-out, and hopefully, avoid them when it's your turn.

-----------------------
Speaker Lineup

Danielle Dalton, Software Engineer
👗Rent the Runway
Tracking customer address changes is a problem for any ecommerce company, particularly a subscription-based business like Rent the Runway. Learn how Rent the Runway is solving this problem with dbt snapshots.

Luc Alvarado, Data Scientist
🚲🛴Lyft Bikes & Scooters
Designing data models to be accessible for business users is a critical component of a data-driven organization. At Lyft Bikes and Scooters, rapid growth and a recent acquisition have added to data complexity, making this task even more challenging. Learn how the team have leveraged mental models to make data more accessible.

Jason Gluck, Data Engineer; Karishma Pradhan, Data Analyst
🛏️Casper
COLLABORATION!!! Good data engineer and data analyst relationships are the hallmark of successful data teams. Learn how engineers and analysts at Casper work together to deploy new data models.
